comment.body: China is just learning from what happened to Russia. Freezing Russian assets abroad and kicking Russia out of SWIFT bcs of Ukraine war was the dumbest thing to do, as it signals to anybody what will happen to their assets in case of a conflict. So China is dumping US treasuries for two reasons: (i) ceasing to finance US defence spending (ii) avoiding having their holdings frozen in case of a Taiwan invasion

comment.body: In the QRA for 1Q2024, announced on 1Nov23 (if I remember correctly), she departed from Treasury's own regulations/guidance on the mix of short term vs long term paper, which conveniently had the effect of injecting a huge amount of liquidity into equities (which otherwise had been earmarked for buying long-term paper, until her QRA).  That and the coordinated actions of several central banks on the same day caused the lock-out rally that followed.  Also avoided what appeared to be at least a much deeper correction in the market which would've been bad politically with election year coming up.  The lesson:  central planning is just as effective and free from error in finance as it is for crops or anything else.
comment.raw_body: In the QRA for 1Q2024, announced on 1Nov23 (if I remember correctly), she departed from Treasury's own regulations/guidance on the mix of short term vs long term paper, which conveniently had the effect of injecting a huge amount of liquidity into equities (which otherwise had been earmarked for buying long-term paper, until her QRA).  That and the coordinated actions of several central banks on the same day caused the lock-out rally that followed.  Also avoided what appeared to be at least a much deeper correction in the market which would've been bad politically with election year coming up.  The lesson:  central planning is just as effective and free from error in finance as it is for crops or anything else.
